title: Turning Bourbon Waste into Energy Storage
summary: Scientists in Kentucky found a way to turn waste from making bourbon into something useful. Bourbon is a type of whiskey that creates a lot of leftover grain and watery mash called stillage. This waste is often thrown away or used for animal feed, but it can be messy and costly. Chemists at the University of Kentucky used a special cooking method with heat and pressure to change the stillage into a black powder. They then heated this powder to make two types of carbon: hard carbon and activated carbon. They used the activated carbon to build devices called supercapacitors, which can store energy like batteries. In tests, these supercapacitors stored as much energy as some commercial ones, and a hybrid version stored even more. This discovery could help reduce waste and create new ways to store energy for things like electronics.

the leftover watery mash from making bourbon
a material made from the waste that can store energy
devices that store energy, similar to batteries

options: It reduces waste and stores energy
questions.correct_answer: It reduces waste and stores energy

background_read: Bourbon is a popular drink made in places like Kentucky. When it's made, there's a lot of leftover grain and watery mash that often goes to waste. Scientists are always looking for ways to turn waste into useful things to help the environment. Energy storage is important for powering devices like phones and cars. This article shows how chemists used a cooking method to change bourbon waste into materials for storing energy, which could make things cleaner and more efficient.
Article_Structure: WHO: Chemists at the University of Kentucky
WHAT: Turned bourbon waste into carbon materials for supercapacitors
WHY: To reduce waste and create better energy storage devices

perspectives.description: This method helps reduce waste from bourbon production, making it more sustainable.
